Let us go in with the story. Imagine you want to know if water will fall from the sky today. You look at a moment when you thought it would rain, but then that dark cloud moves away and water falls too. Your thought was right about what would happen, but the reason you had for thinking it would rain was because you saw a black cat. The black cat has nothing to do with the rain. They are just two things that happened at the same time. Yet, what if the black cat did not make it rain? What if there was no wind coming that made both of them wet? In this case, your first thought was wrong because you were looking at something that does not matter. Second, we must look at how we got to our thoughts. Many people think things in their heads that are true or make things to happen. When you think something, that thing has a special power to stay true or stay true. Third, we see that these two ways of being right are not enough to say that someone knows something. If you have a right thought by chance, you do not have the thing that comes from knowing. Knowing needs that your way of getting to the truth is strong and works every time. If your way of finding the truth is just a lucky guess or a wrong way of looking at things, then you do not really know. You are simply hitting the right mark by chance.
